title: FOIA Records: ICE Knew of 353% Use-of-Force Surge in Early 2025, Took No Action
excerpt: Internal DHS emails obtained by watchdog group American Oversight revealed that ICE leadership was briefed in early 2025 on a 353 percent surge in reported use-of-force incidents compared to the prior year. Rather than addressing the spike, senior officials focused on prosecuting civilians for assaulting agents.
content:
On February 17–18, 2026, American Oversight published internal DHS emails and documents obtained through a FOIA lawsuit revealing that ICE leadership had been informed of a dramatic surge in use-of-force incidents months before the fatal shootings in Minneapolis drew national attention.

  • In the first two months of Trump's second administration, DHS documented a 353 percent increase in use-of-force incidents compared to the prior year, with 67 incidents reported between January and early March 2025 versus 17 during the same period in 2024.
  • The official overseeing ICE field operations was formally notified on March 20, 2025, that officers' use of force was up nearly fourfold year-over-year.
  • Documents showed 10 reports of officers shattering car windows, deploying chemical agents, and using extreme physical force, including one report detailing the fatal shooting of U.S. citizen Ruben Ray Martinez in Texas.
  • Rather than addressing the increased violence by agents, senior ICE officials discussed ways to increase prosecutions of civilians for assaults against officers, with no parallel effort to discipline or retrain agents.
  • American Oversight Executive Director Chioma Chukwu stated the records showed a 'stark disconnect between the constitutional standards on which ICE claims to train its officers and the abusive and deadly enforcement practices' on the ground.
'ICE knows its practices are deeply problematic — and is deliberately hiding the ball to avoid public scrutiny.' — American Oversight Executive Director Chioma Chukwu, quoted in Politico
mediaPlaceholder: Internal ICE emails from March 2025 detailing the use-of-force surge briefing to acting ICE Director
